title: Esoteric stuff in 10 20 30 40
wikitext:
[]: Esoteric stuff in 1.0/2.0/3.0/4.0             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sat Feb 09, 2008 10:20 am --

[]:             Quote Post by Jumpstart A. Cat » Sat Feb 09, 2008 7:19 pm It's 'Khara' written in Greek. The letters are Chi, alpha, rho, alpha. The 'X with a tail' isn't especially significant, it's just the letter Chi. And the p is rho. The 'fish' are the small letter alpha. I don't think it's particularly significant. I would guess that the reason the logo is written in Greek is because 'Neon Genesis Evangelion' is Greek.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Feb 09, 2008 8:09 pm No, Zugzwang's correct about the origins of this. Chi-Rho. Two fish. ICHTHYOS- Iesus Christos Theos Yoes Soter- Jesus Christ God's Son Savior. That and the Pisces association are why the fish is an early Christian emblem.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Feb 10, 2008 7:46 am --

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Feb 10, 2008 9:34 pm Zugzwang wrote: Jumpstart A. Cat wrote: The 'X with a tail' isn't especially significant, it's just the letter Chi.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Mon Feb 11, 2008 9:10 am --

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Feb 11, 2008 10:07 am Zugzwang wrote: The Marathon one has a curve at either end of the same arm. If you compare it to the example I posted it's still nothing like it, only present on one arm at a right angle and equal to the length of one arm - which makes me think it serves a dual purpose:

[]:             Quote Post by Jumpstart A. Cat » Tue Feb 12, 2008 10:33 am Zugzwang wrote: Show me an example. You see x written with small tails on either end or on all four arms all the time in maths or physics books, but I've never seen an example of the tail at a right-angle and only present on one arm of the x.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Feb 12, 2008 11:04 am --

[]:             Quote Post by Jumpstart A. Cat » Tue Feb 12, 2008 11:25 am Zugzwang wrote: Do you have an actual example you can show me, from a book or website about chemistry?

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Feb 12, 2008 12:32 pm --

[]:             Quote Post by Jumpstart A. Cat » Wed Feb 13, 2008 12:49 pm Zugzwang wrote: The other image. There were other letters around the outside written upsidedown and at right-angles to the rest of the image that I removed, just to make it clearer what I was on about as they made it kind of busy to look at.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Feb 13, 2008 3:20 pm --

[]:             Quote Post by Jumpstart A. Cat » Thu Feb 14, 2008 9:09 am Zugzwang wrote: Problem here is I wasn't talking about the X itself being a perfect X, each arm being at a 90 degree angle to each other --- I was talking about the single tail being at a 90 degree angle in relation to the arm it's on in the example I posted, because all the Greek Xs I have seen had curved tails leading off each arm of the X, and none of them were at a right-angle and of equal length to one arm of the X.

[]:             Quote Post by Maverick » Wed Feb 27, 2008 2:04 am Ahhh, good 'n old Evangelion. Even the studio's name leads to lenghty discussions among eva fans =) (by the way, I've just watched 1.0... it's incredible how can just watching the series again awaken all those mixed feelings that were long forgotten... eva is strange... really... )

[]:             Quote Post by sedemihcrA » Thu Mar 06, 2008 4:49 pm The masonic symbol is probably just an allusion to SEELE's status as a secret society. They already have a pretty heavy Illuminati-feel to them in the original series. I predict this will be one of many symbols that goes completely unexplained over the course of the series. Annoying but at least we know to expect Pointless Symbolisms this time around. Probably in the same way that the rainbows don't actually mean anything (they just look pretty.)

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Mar 06, 2008 5:14 pm That is not Chi, it is Kappa. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kappa_%28letter%29 What happened is that they spelled it using an odd variant of the Kappa symbol, ("K"). They did not use Chi, which is a straight "X". l.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 06, 2008 6:59 pm Khara is most certainly spelled with a "chi". Japanese sites, including the official ones, have spelled the name out in its Greek letters (in addition to the katakana), and the intended meaning of the word has been supplied somewhere or other. Khara can be found in a Greek dictionary exactly as promised.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Mar 06, 2008 8:19 pm Yes, on closer inspection agreed. However, "Chr-ist" and "Kh-a-R-a" just both happend to need a "chi" sound and an "r" sound, and this does not seem to have a particular connection.

[]:             Quote Post by Lord Glavin » Fri Mar 07, 2008 2:10 am well it doesn't look like anyone mentioned this but 'xapa' in english is 'happiness'
